title: Sudurpaschim Province - The Kathmandu Post
summary: Two dead, four injured in Darchula jeep accident By Manoj Badu Police say the vehicle carrying irrigation office staff crashed in Shailyashikhar Municipality on Tuesday morning. Janak Giri wins Bajura seat By Arjun Shah The Congress candidate defeats UML’s Lal Bahadur Thapa by 540 votes. Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Gyanendra Mahata leads in Kanchanpur-3 By Post Report Preliminary count shows RSP ca
content:
Two dead, four injured in Darchula jeep accident By Manoj Badu Police say the vehicle carrying irrigation office staff crashed in Shailyashikhar Municipality on Tuesday morning. Janak Giri wins Bajura seat By Arjun Shah The Congress candidate defeats UML’s Lal Bahadur Thapa by 540 votes. Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Gyanendra Mahata leads in Kanchanpur-3 By Post Report Preliminary count shows RSP candidate ahead. Outmigration empties Bajhang settlements, cuts voter numbers By Basant Pratap Singh Most families have moved to India cities for work, and the villages have mostly elderly residents and children. Clash between UML and RSP cadres in Baitadi leaves three injured By Mohan Chand Clash erupts during door-to-door campaign in Dilasaini Rural Municipality. Tiger census in Shuklaphanta National Park to start March 7 By Post Report Automated cameras will monitor the park and surrounding reserves to update big cat numbers. Election cop dies in Baitadi By Mohan Chand Third death of election police personnel in the district during current deployment. Women serve as election police, men handle home duties By Menuka Dhungana In Achham, temporary election work has given women both a source of income and a boost in confidence. Traditional gender roles are slowly changing. Indian youth head to Achham for work as locals continue to leave for India By Menuka Dhungana Cross-border labour trend grows as Indian workers take up gabion weaving jobs in western Nepal. Tiger count in Shuklaphanta to begin after polls By Bhawani Bhatta Survey deferred as park staff mobilised for election duty; 260 grids identified for camera installation. Youth killed in muzzleloader misfire in Doti By Barun Paneru Police say incident occurred while electrical wiring was underway in a house in Bogatan Fudsil Rural Municipality Displaced and on the edge of forests, families face daily threats from wildlife By Bhawani Bhatta Elephant intrusions, destroyed crops, and occasional deaths are part of daily life for hundreds of families in Dhakka. Daily bread beats ballot for Nepali youths on seasonal migration By Chitranga Thapa Most say they would vote if they could, but missing work in India could cost them a year’s income. Sudurpaschim govt to provide Rs100,000 to families of Baitadi bus crash victims By Dr Tek Raj Joshi Government to bear treatment costs of injured. Baitadi wedding bus crash survivor recounts narrow escape as 13 killed By Mohan Chand One of the injured passengers describes jumping from the overcrowded bus. Livestock farming in crisis as China tightens cross-border grazing By Basant Pratap Singh Nepali herders are facing harassment in Tibet despite a 2012 deal to allow grazing within 30 km on both sides. Balendra Shah completes Sudurpaschim tour By Menuka Dhungana Senior RSP leader adopts brief, symbolic visits in all 16 constituencies of the province. From Tarai to the hills: Election police recruits grapple with Kalikot terrain By Tularam Pandey Young applicants drawn to Kalikot by lower competition say steep roads and cold define their first experience of the hills. Families of two slain girls still awaiting justice a year on By Menuka Dhungana They were brutally murdered by two boys after one girl refused sexual advances. Darchula college students demand maths teacher as exams approach By Bhawani Bhatta After two years of protests were unheeded, Gokuleshwar Multiple Campus students take their demand to Far Western University in Mahendranagar.

By Manoj Badu
